entry.title: Exploring dates and times in Python
entry.content: Fun with dates and times in Python
entry.summary: Fun with dates and times in Python

entry.title: Wednesday OT Birders - Using the ebird API, Python, and R to analyze data for our birding group
entry.content: Using the eBird API from Python, we downloaded birding lists submitted by our Wednesday morning birding group. R was then used to create some basic plots showing the top species sighted each year. More analysis to follow.
entry.summary: Using the eBird API from Python, we downloaded birding lists submitted by our Wednesday morning birding group. R was then used to create some basic plots showing the top species sighted each year. More analysis to follow.

entry.title: Computing daily averages from transaction data using pandas can be tricky - Part 1
entry.content: Our goal is to compute statistics such as the average number of bikes rented per day in the Pronto CycleShare dataset, both overall and for individual stations. Seems easy. We’ll see.
entry.summary: Our goal is to compute statistics such as the average number of bikes rented per day in the Pronto CycleShare dataset, both overall and for individual stations. Seems easy. We’ll see.

entry.title: Computing daily averages from transaction data - Part 2 (using pandas and some R magic)
entry.content: In Part 2 of this post, we’ll extend this to cases in which we might want to group by an additional field (e.g. station_id for the bike share example) and see how this problem is just one part of the general problem of doing occupancy analysis based on transaction data.
entry.summary: In Part 2 of this post, we’ll extend this to cases in which we might want to group by an additional field (e.g. station_id for the bike share example) and see how this problem is just one part of the general problem of doing occupancy analysis based on transaction data.

entry.title: Use R to explore station balance in bike share systems - and learn a little dplyr programming

entry.title: Wednesday Birders - 2018 stats and historical analysis using R
entry.content: Using the eBird API from Python, we downloaded birding lists submitted by our Wednesday morning birding group. R was then used to produce various plots relating to counts of birds sighted and frequency of list appearance, by species.
entry.summary: Using the eBird API from Python, we downloaded birding lists submitted by our Wednesday morning birding group. R was then used to produce various plots relating to counts of birds sighted and frequency of list appearance, by species.

entry.title: Using hillmaker from R with reticulate to analyze time of day patterns in bike share data
entry.content: Python based hillmaker is called from R using reticulate.
entry.summary: Python based hillmaker is called from R using reticulate.

entry.title: Using R to download and plot Great Lakes historical water level data
entry.content: This year we have seen record or near record high water levels in all of the Great Lakes. While plots of historical water levels are available on the web, I thought it would be interesting create my own plots using R. In this post I will summarize the steps for using R to:
entry.summary: This year we have seen record or near record high water levels in all of the Great Lakes. While plots of historical water levels are available on the web, I thought it would be interesting create my own plots using R. In this post I will summarize the steps for using R to:
